---
title: "effective_curve"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Source:** [`plotting/with_plotly/effective_curve.py`](https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools/blob/main/plotting/with_plotly/effective_curve.py)
:::


## API Reference

#### `get_figure` {#get_figure}

```python
def get_figure(t: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, dy: np.ndarray, title: str, yaxis_title: str, labels: List[str], legend_title: str, colors_rgb=List[str], tmin=None, tmax=None, ymin=None, ymax=None, t1_fit=None, t2_fit=None, y_fit=None, dy_fit=None, width=phi_golden * 500, height=500)
```

Plot the correlators effective curves y_i(t) as a function of t (in lattice units)
If  t1_fit, t2_fit, y_fit, dy_fit are passed, the fit band is plotted for each trace.

[View source ↗](https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools/blob/main/plotting/with_plotly/effective_curve.py#L10){.source-link style='font-size:0.8em;'}

---
